articles.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims

articles.title: Why Andrew Yang is building instead of waiting for Washington
articles.description: Andrew Yang’s 2020 presidential campaign was based on a warning that automation and AI would hollow out the labor market and concentrate wealth in the hands of a few. At the time, ideas like Universal Basic Income felt fringe. Now Dario Amodei, Sam Altman, and Bernie Sanders are all saying versions of the same thing.  An entrepreneur at heart, […]

articles.title: Cybercriminals claim breach of Oracle PeopleSoft servers at 100-plus organizations
articles.description: The ShinyHunters hacking gang claims to have compromised the Oracle PeopleSoft servers of more than 100 organizations, including many universities.

articles.title: Everyone wants a piece of Tesla’s battery business
articles.description: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

articles.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
articles.description: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

articles.title: North Koreans behind nearly half of US tech industry hacks, says CrowdStrike
articles.description: North Korean hackers posing as remote IT workers and recruiters remain a major threat to U.S., European, and Asian companies, accounting for about half of all attacks over the past 12 months.

articles.title: Wing drone delivery might not be a novelty anymore

articles.title: Netflix expands revamped mobile app across Asia and doubles down on kids’ gaming

articles.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
articles.description: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

articles.title: The Indian government got cold feet on Starlink just before SpaceX’s IPO
articles.description: Problems with Starlink's India expansion could challenge SpaceX's IPO growth story.

articles.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
articles.description: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

articles.title: Zest launches a restaurant discovery app powered by where people actually eat
articles.description: Backed by Alexis Ohanian’s 776 and Kindred Ventures, Zest uses transaction data and AI to generate restaurant recommendations based on users’ real dining habits and the places they frequent.

articles.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
articles.description: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

articles.title: Why enterprise AI will be a major focus at VivaTech 2026
articles.description: While Silicon Valley continues pushing aggressively into large language models and consumer-facing AI products, many European companies are focused on applying AI to complex systems already embedded into everyday life.

articles.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
articles.description: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

articles.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
articles.description: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

articles.title: Pinterest bets on creators with Amazon Storefront integration
articles.description: Pinterest is adding support for Amazon Storefronts, allowing creators to earn affiliate commissions more easily while showcasing their product recommendations in one place.

articles.description: ServiceNow is used by thousands of enterprises to automate their internal processes, but says several customers had data accessed because of a security bug.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI安全与争议**：xAI因工程师对Grok安全性提出警告而将其解雇，引发新诉讼；同时，Anthropic的Fable工具因安全限制引发网络安全研究者不满。
2. **网络安全威胁加剧**：网络犯罪分子声称入侵了100多家机构的Oracle PeopleSoft服务器；CrowdStrike报告称，美国科技行业近半数黑客攻击与朝鲜有关。
3. **科技巨头持续投资AI**：亚马逊刚完成债券销售后，又向银行借款175亿美元用于AI支出；企业每月为每位员工在AI上花费7500美元，显示出“AI上瘾”趋势。
4. **新兴应用与市场动态**：Wing无人机送货不再新奇；Netflix在亚洲扩展改版移动应用并加码儿童游戏；Zest推出基于真实就餐数据的餐厅发现应用；Datadog资深员工创立AI编码初创公司Niteshift，挑战大型AI锁定。
5. **政策与商业博弈**：印度政府在SpaceX IPO前夕对Starlink态度转冷；Andrew Yang选择自行建设而非等待华盛顿行动；VivaTech 2026将聚焦企业AI。

今日最值得关注的发展：AI安全与商业利益之间的冲突日益尖锐，同时网络安全威胁的规模与来源（如朝鲜黑客）正显著升级，成为科技行业不可忽视的双重挑战。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **语音代理与多语言处理**：研究团队对前沿自动语音识别（ASR）系统在代码切换（双语混合）场景下的表现进行了基准测试，探讨语音代理处理双语用户的能力。
2. **开发者工具与模型发布**：Cohere推出面向开发者的首个模型“North Mini Code”；Hugging Face发布多项更新，包括通过链接两个Spaces构建3D巴黎画廊的Agent案例、将GitHub CI迁移至Hugging Face Jobs、以及设计面向Agent优化的hf CLI工具。
3. **开源与安全进展**：开源社区支持用于强化学习的OpenEnv框架；NVIDIA推出Nemotron 3.5内容安全模型，提供可定制的多模态安全方案，适用于企业级全球AI部署。
4. **企业AI应用与合作**：LSEG（伦敦证券交易所集团）利用AI实现从数据到决策的规模化信任；Nextdoor和Notion工程师通过OpenAI Codex提升开发效率；OpenAI宣布可通过Oracle云承诺访问其模型及Codex。
5. **政策与地缘安全**：OpenAI披露有与中国关联的影响力行动试图干预美国AI辩论；同时发布“智能时代产业政策”相关文章，并确认已向SEC秘密提交S-1草案（IPO准备）。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：多家机构密集发布面向开发者和企业的AI工具与模型（如Cohere的North Mini Code、Nemotron 3.5安全模型），同时OpenAI在商业合作、政策影响及上市进程上同步推进，显示AI行业正从技术研发向规模化落地与合规治理加速转型。
